name: Mrs. f Double-Dipped Chocolate Shortbread Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the semisweet chocolate over hot (not simmering) water in a double boiler. Set aside to cool to lukewarm.
2. In a large bowl, cream the butter with an electric mixer. Beat in the melted chocolate until well combined.
3. Add the vanilla extract, flour, confectioners' sugar, cocoa powder, and salt. Mix until just combined.
4. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes or until firm enough to roll into balls.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Roll the dough into 1-inch balls and then roll each ball into a thick log.
7. Place the logs on an ungreased cookie sheet, pressing them down so they are about 1/4 inch thick and oval in shape.
8. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until just set. Do not overbake.
9. Transfer to wire racks to cool.
Dipping Cookies:
1. In a small saucepan set over hot water, melt the white chocolate with 1/2 cup heavy cream. Stir until smooth and keep warm over simmering water.
2. In another small saucepan set over hot water, melt the semisweet chocolate with the remaining 1/2 cup heavy cream. Stir until smooth and keep warm over simmering water.
3. Dip one end of a cookie into the white chocolate and the other end into the dark chocolate. Return to cooling racks so the chocolate can set.
4. Repeat with the remaining cookies.
